_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.shape: The yield curve represents Treasury yields across maturities from 1 month to 30 years, with a normal upward slope indicating higher yields for longer maturities due to risk and time preferences. An inverted yield curve, where short-term yields exceed long-term yields, has historically preceded every modern US recession by 6 to 18 months. Currently, the 10-year Treasury yield stands at 4.48% for June 2026, and the 10-year to 2-year (2s10s) spread is 0.35 points, reflecting an upward sloping yield curve. This contrasts with the flat or inverted curve seen from 2022 through 2024, when the 2s10s spread was below zero, representing the period of deepest inversion at 0.0 points.
notes.signal: The 2s10s spread, a key recession indicator measuring the difference between 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields, spent 2022 to 2024 inverted, signaling caution as inversion has historically preceded recessions. The recent re-steepening of the curve above zero to 0.35 points suggests a partial normalization or 'un-inversion' often observed shortly before recessions arrive. Meanwhile, the New York Fed’s recession probability stands at 31%, reflecting a moderately elevated risk but not a certainty of recession. The combination of the negative-to-positive spread transition and the 31% recession probability suggests caution but does not confirm imminent recession timing due to the curve’s known pre-recession dynamics.
notes.now: Currently, the yield curve's upward slope at 0.35 points after a period of inversion indicates some normalization, yet the elevated New York Fed recession probability of 31% suggests persistent underlying economic risks. The data signals caution without specifying when a downturn might occur, consistent with the yield curve’s historical role as a leading but not precise timing indicator.
